description:
Structural epic to make fz's three execution paths (interpreter, JIT, AOT) real and testable, with feedback-loop tooling and a fixture-driven workflow.

## Background

Audit on 2026-05-11 found:

- Only the JIT path is alive. AOT (src/aot.rs) is `#![cfg(any())]`-gated and not even `mod`-declared. fz build is a stub that exits 2.
- Two parallel "interpreters" exist with unclear roles. eval.rs::Interp is really a compile-time AST evaluator (used by macros / repl / test_runner). ir_interp.rs is declared but has zero production consumers, walks IR but uses value::Value (not the JIT's FzValue), so it can't act as a JIT oracle even if it were wired in.
- ~4,200 lines of pre-IR direct-style code preserved as orphan files (codegen.rs, jit.rs, aot.rs, test_support.rs). Their stated preservation rationale (.11.9 cutover) expired long ago.
- "Three-path parity" (per feedback memory) is aspirational, not enforced.
- No CLIF/asm dump tooling, so iterating on perf or correctness of generated code requires reading source.
- Fixtures exist but lack a uniform check protocol or index.

## Target structure

  parser → ast.rs → resolve → macros → ast (canonical)
                                          │
                                       ir_lower → fz_ir::Module → ir_typer
                                          │
                                ┌─────────┼─────────┐
                              interp    jit       aot
                                └─────────┼─────────┘
                                          │
                            shared runtime substrate:
                            FzValue + heap + Process + Runtime/scheduler + FFI

Properties:
1. One IR, one typer — all three backends consume the same `fz_ir::Module`.
2. One value rep at runtime — FzValue + heap, on every path.
3. One runtime ABI — spawn/send/receive/alloc live in runtime.rs; JIT calls via FFI, AOT links staticlib, interp calls plain Rust. Concurrency works on every path without reimplementation.

## Goals

G1. Mechanical cleanup. Delete the four orphan files. Decide fz build's fate. Rename eval.rs to make its compile-time AST-evaluator role explicit.

G2. Fixture-first discipline. Every fixture has a header (`# purpose:` and `# paths:`) and an optional sidecar `.expected` with stdout. Exit 0 = pass, nonzero = fail. `cargo test fixture_matrix` walks fixtures/, runs each through the listed paths, diffs against `.expected`. `--bless` updates expectations. fixtures/index.md is regenerated from headers; stale index fails the build.

G3. Feedback-loop tooling. `fz dump <file> [--fn name] --emit clif|asm|both` produces source-annotated dumps (CLIF text and post-regalloc machine code with `; @file:line:col` interleaved). Same flag on `fz run`. Implementation reuses Cranelift's SourceLoc + capstone; spans are already threaded through ir_codegen (fz-ul4.21).

G4. Rebuilt ir_interp on the runtime substrate. Consumes the same fz_ir::Module as the JIT, uses FzValue + heap, calls runtime.rs FFI for spawn/send/receive. Subsumes fz-ul4.19.8. Real interp at last; macros/REPL/test_runner migrate off the AST evaluator for runtime concerns.

G5. AOT path on factored Cranelift core. Extract jit-agnostic codegen from ir_codegen.rs into a backend core, parameterized on cranelift_module::Module. JIT picks JITModule; AOT picks ObjectModule + entry-point shim that initializes Runtime. Both gain `--emit` uniformly. Closes fz-ul4.22.

## Non-goals

- stderr matching in fixtures (simple stdout-only for now)
- CLIF/asm golden files (drift across Cranelift versions / platforms)
- Polyglot AOT targets / cross-compile
- Removing the AST evaluator entirely (it's needed for macro expansion)

## Execution order

Phase 1 — Fixture runner + sidecar `.expected` + index.md regen + headers on existing fixtures (JIT-only paths to start). Makes everything else testable.

Phase 2 — Mechanical cleanup. Delete orphans, rename eval.rs, decide fz build. Free wins, no behavior change.

Phase 3 — `fz dump --emit clif|asm`. Source-annotated dumps. Real feedback loop.

Phase 4 — Factor ir_codegen into backend core + JIT driver. Prep for AOT and interp parity.

Phase 5 — Rebuild ir_interp on FzValue/heap/runtime substrate. Wire up the `interp` fixture path. Migrate REPL/test_runner.

Phase 6 — AOT driver on the factored core. Wire up the `aot` fixture path.

## Acceptance

- Every fixture in fixtures/ runs cleanly through every listed path via `cargo test fixture_matrix`.
- fixtures/index.md is auto-regenerated; CI fails on drift.
- `fz dump --emit clif --fn add fixtures/add1.fz` prints source-annotated CLIF.
- src/aot.rs / src/jit.rs / src/codegen.rs / src/test_support.rs are deleted (history preserves them).
- The new feature workflow is: write fixture → fail → make pass → mark all three paths green.

## CLAUDE.md alignment

- TDD is the norm → fixture-first IS the TDD loop.
- Warnings are errors / Tests must pass → enforced by the matrix runner.
- Deferrals require authorization → enforce via `# paths:` declarations; any feature not on all three paths is an explicit deferral named in the fixture.
- Tickets are memory → this epic and its children.
- Elegance is the rule → factor over duplicate; one IR, one value rep, one runtime ABI.
title: [EPIC] Three-path parity, feedback loops, fixture-first discipline
